curriculum_excerpts.text: 物质是由分子、原子构成的，物质结构决定性质，物质性质决定用途。
能通过实例认识物质的性质与应用的关系，形成合理利用物质的意识。
认识物质性质的思路与方法。
（1）展示金刚石、石墨、C₆₀的实物或高清图片，形成直观对比。
（3）联系铅笔芯（石墨）、钻石饰品（金刚石）、纳米材料（C₆₀）等日常事物。
能解释金刚石硬度大而石墨质地软的原因（原子排列方式不同）；能写出碳单质在高温下与氧气、氧化铜等物质反应的化学方程式；能举例说明碳单质在生活和生产中的广泛应用.

Potassium and magnesium are required in our diet. Write the electron configurations of the ions expected
from these elements.
Answer: K+: [Ar], Mg2+: [Ne]

exercises.stem: 依据课标「碳的单质（金刚石/石墨/C60）」：物质是由分子、原子构成的，物质结构决定性质，物质性质决定用途。 请简要阐述并举例。
exercises.answer: 应答覆盖课标要点，体现掌握与应用。

exercises.stem: 比较说明：能通过实例认识物质的性质与应用的关系，形成合理利用物质的意识。
exercises.answer: 需写出关键概念、过程或方法，避免只背结论。

common_errors.text: 只记方程式不说明反应条件与类型
忽视实验操作与安全意识

This image appears on page 567 of the textbook. ![Figure 440 - A figure or diagram (650×333px) related to: Figure, covalent](OpenStax_Chemistry_images/img_0440_c8551e7f.png) the crystal all have the same strength and all require the same amount of energy to be broken. The gradual softening of an amorphous material differs dramatically from the distinct melting of a crystalline solid. This results from the structural nonequivalence of the molecules in the amorphous solid. Some forces are weaker than others, and when an amorphous material is heated, the weakest intermolecular attractions break first. As the temperature is increased further, the stronger attractions are broken. Thus amorphous materials soften over a range of temperatures. The relationship between the vapor pressures of solution components and the concentrations of those components is described by Raoult’s law: The partial pressure exerted by any component of an ideal solution is equal to the vapor pressure of the pure component multiplied by its mole fraction in the solution.

For example, combustion of a fuel in air involves transfer of heat from a system (the fuel and oxygen molecules undergoing reaction) to surroundings that are infinitely more massive (the earth’s atmosphere). As a result, qsurr is a good approximation of qrev, and the second law may be stated as the following: ΔSuniv = ΔSsys + ΔSsurr = ΔSsys + qsurr T We may use this equation to predict the spontaneity of a process as illustrated in Example 16.4. Example 16.4 Will Ice Spontaneously Melt? The entropy change for the process H2 O(s) ⟶H2 O(l) is 22.1 J/K and requires that the surroundings transfer 6.00 kJ of heat to the system. Figure 18.28 Before the fleet’s retirement in 2011, liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen were used in the three main engines of a space shuttle. Two compartments in the large tank held these liquids until the shuttle was launched. (credit: “reynermedia”/Flickr) An uncombined hydrogen atom consists of a nucleus and one valence electron in the 1s orbital. The n = 1 valence shell has a capacity for two electrons, and hydrogen can rightfully occupy two locations in the periodic table. It is possible to consider hydrogen a group 1 element because hydrogen can lose an electron to form the cation, H+. It is also possible to consider hydrogen to be a group 17 element because it needs only one electron to fill its valence orbital to form a hydride ion, H−, or it can share an electron to form a single, covalent bond. In reality, hydrogen is a unique element that almost deserves its own location in the periodic table. Reactions with Elements When heated, hydrogen reacts with the metals of group 1 and with Ca, Sr, and Ba (the more active metals in group 2). The compounds formed are crystalline, ionic hydrides that contain the hydride anion, H−, a strong reducing agent and a strong base, which reacts vigorously with water and other acids to form hydrogen gas. The reactions of hydrogen with nonmetals generally produce acidic hydrogen compounds with hydrogen in the 1+ oxidation state. The reactions become more exothermic and vigorous as the electronegativity of the nonmetal increases. Hydrogen reacts with nitrogen and sulfur only when heated, but it reacts explosively with fluorine (forming

## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
碳的单质（金刚石/石墨/C60）是化学学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“物质是由分子、原子构成的，物质结构决定性质，物质性质决定用途。”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：从实验现象、微观粒子和符号表达三个层面讲解「碳的单质（金刚石/石墨/C60）」，让学生把现象与本质对应起来。
2. 探究建构：重视实验安全、证据记录、化学用语规范和条件限制，避免只背结论。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“物质是由分子、原子构成的，物质结构决定性质，物质性质决定用途。”，安排观察、解释、书写表达和应用判断任务。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。
